description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses a global before_script to set up SSH authentication: install openssh-client if missing, start ssh-agent, create the ~/.ssh directory, decrypt the private key with $encrypted_key and $encrypted_iv into ~/.ssh/publish-key, set its permissions, configure SSH to use this identity, and add the Pantheon host key to ~/.ssh/known_hosts. Define a single job called deploy_pantheon that runs in the default stage and is limited to the master branch (only: - master). In this job, run two script commands: add a git remote named pantheon pointing to the Pantheon SSH URL (ssh://codeserver.dev.21dc5d71-9de1-4fa9-93e8-a45cd3a27c27@codeserver.dev.21dc5d71-9de1-4fa9-93e8-a45cd3a27c27.drush.in:2222/~/repository.git) and push the master branch to that remote. No specific Docker image, services, caches, artifacts, or additional deployment targets are defined beyond the SSH push to Pantheon.
